---
title: "Cleaning a Parkinson's Disease Microbiome Data"
author:
    - name: Longhai Li
      url: https://longhaisk.github.io
date: last-modified
execute: 
  cache: false
  freeze: false
  warning: false
  message: false
params:
  clearresults: FALSE
  seed: 400
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    code-fold: true
    number-sections: true
    cache: false
    freeze: false
  typst: 
    echo: false
    cache: false
    freeze: false
    fig-format: pdf
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---


## Data Loading, Cleaning and Diagnostics

```{r}
#| cache: false
#| label: data-cleaning
library(dplyr)

if (!file.exists(cleaned_data_file)) {
  dataset <- read.csv("HillsBurnPD.csv", stringsAsFactors=TRUE)

  print(paste("Total starting rows:", nrow(dataset)))
  print(paste("Rows with < 5000 reads:", sum(dataset$Total_reads < 5000, na.rm = TRUE)))
  summary(dataset %>% select(sex, age, parkinson_disease))

  duplicates_to_drop <- c("10567.EC0104201B", "10567.EP0201201B", "10567.GMWA.1067B")

  shorten_taxa_names_robust <- function(taxa_strings, prefix = "otu_") {
    shortened_names <- sapply(taxa_strings, function(x) {
      if (grepl("Unassigned", x, ignore.case = TRUE)) return("Unassigned")
      matches <- regmatches(x, gregexpr("[kpcofgs]__[\\._]*[A-Za-z0-9-]+", x))[[1]]
      if (length(matches) > 0) {
        last_match <- tail(matches, 1)
        clean_match <- sub("([kpcofgs]__)[\\._]+", "\\1", last_match)
        return(clean_match)
      } else {
        return(x)
      }
    })
    return(paste0(prefix, shortened_names))
  }

  cleaned_dataset <- dataset %>%
    filter(Total_reads >= 5000) %>%
    filter(!sample_name %in% duplicates_to_drop) %>%
    rename_with(
      ~ make.unique(shorten_taxa_names_robust(.)), 
      starts_with("k__") | starts_with("Unassigned")
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(starts_with("otu_", ignore.case = FALSE), ~ asin(sqrt(.x / Total_reads)))) %>%
    mutate(
      parkinson_disease = factor(tolower(parkinson_disease)),
      sex = ifelse(tolower(sex) == "female", 1, 0),
      sex_age = sex * age  
    ) %>%
    select(parkinson_disease, sex, age, sex_age, starts_with("otu_", ignore.case = FALSE))

  print(paste("Final sample count:", nrow(cleaned_dataset)))
  saveRDS(cleaned_dataset, file = cleaned_data_file)
} else {
  cleaned_dataset <- readRDS(cleaned_data_file)
}

# 1. Extract the response vector (y)
y <- cleaned_dataset$parkinson_disease
Y_numeric <- as.numeric(y == "yes")

# 2. Prepare the predictor dataframe (X_df) and convert directly to matrix
X_df <- cleaned_dataset %>% select(-parkinson_disease)
X <- as.matrix(X_df)

# ==========================================
# --- DATA SPLITTING FOR ORDERING/EVAL ---
# ==========================================

set.seed(seed) # Ensure strict reproducibility of the split
idx_ord <- sample(1:nrow(X), n_ord)

# Create the Ordering Set
X_ord <- X[idx_ord, ]
Y_ord <- Y_numeric[idx_ord]

# Create the Evaluation Set
X_eval <- X[-idx_ord, ]
Y_eval <- Y_numeric[-idx_ord]

print(paste("Dimensions of X (Full):", nrow(X), "rows,", ncol(X), "columns"))
print(paste("Ordering Set:", nrow(X_ord), "cases"))
print(paste("Evaluation Set:", nrow(X_eval), "cases"))
```

### Information of the data

```{r}
library(dplyr)
cleaned_dataset %>%
  group_by(parkinson_disease) %>%
  summarize(
    Mean_Age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median_Age = median(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_Age = min(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Age = max(age, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
hist(cleaned_dataset$age, 
     main = "Distribution of Age in PD Dataset", 
     xlab = "Age", 
     col = "lightblue", 
     breaks = 20)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

cleaned_dataset %>%
  count(parkinson_disease, sex) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)
```
